timeline.label: FY2025 audited annual filed (Grant Thornton); net loss $28.9M after NRV and flood charges; dividend $17.50/sh maintained
FY2025: notes payable drawn to $150M (from $36M); revolver capacity expanded to $200M + $80M facilities
FY2024 peak earnings: net income $46.7M; dividend $20/sh
Historic rainfall and flood costs; $38.7M flood charge in FY2024 cost of sales (prior-year context)
Documented transfer/sale of 102,000+ acre-feet surface water out of Kings County since 2009 (journalism/DWR records)
J.G. Boswell Company founded; family control persists; OTC Pink listing with thin float

industry.structure: Large-scale irrigated California cotton, tomato, and grain production with integrated ginning; subsurface mineral interests; highly concentrated family ownership and thin OTC liquidity.
industry.share_shift: Boswell scale and senior San Joaquin/Tulare basin water rights create local cost advantage versus smaller valley operators; no public peer set for direct share comparison.
industry.trend: SGMA groundwater regulation, surface-water scarcity pricing, and cyclical commodity/NRV volatility drive earnings swings; water rights monetization remains episodic rather than contracted.
